title: Support symlinks for quadlet container files
body:
### Feature request description

I'd like to manage my quadlet files in ~/.config/containers/systemd as symlinks to another folder.

Currently, using symlinks results in output similar to this:
```
/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator --user --dryrun
quadlet-generator[3515]: Loading source unit file /home/rafael/.config/containers/systemd/hedgedoc-db.volume
quadlet-generator[3515]: Loading source unit file /home/rafael/.config/containers/systemd/hedgedoc-uploads.volume
quadlet-generator[3515]: Loading source unit file /home/rafael/.config/containers/systemd/hedgedoc.container
quadlet-generator[3515]: No files parsed from [/home/rafael/.config/containers/systemd /etc/containers/systemd/users/1000 /etc/containers/systemd/users]
```

### Suggest potential solution

I'd like quadlet to successfully follow and read contents of symlinks.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Using hardlinks instead of symlinks does not work for me.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: @raffomania Do you have a some files and some symlinks?  If all symlinks to the same directory, you could bind mount the directory.  Not sure why symlinks should not be followed though.

The following worked for me:
```
echo "[Volume]" > /tmp/real.volume
ln -s /tmp/real.volume ~/.config/containers/systemd/
/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator --user --dryrun
```

Are you symlinking each file separately or at the directory level? Could this be a permissions (or SELinux) issue?

Apologies. It turns out I forgot to use the `-r` flag, resulting in broken relative symlinks. With correct symlinks, everything actually works fine!

Thanks for the help and sorry to take up your time!
